details: The scheme “Medical Check-Up Facility” is implemented by the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board (UKBOCWWB) under the Labour Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide medical check-up facilities to registered construction workers through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.
benefits: Under this scheme, medical check-up facilities are provided to construction workers.
eligibility: The applicant must be a construction worker registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.

offline: To provide medical check-up benefits as part of social security to construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board, medical check-up facilities are offered through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.

documents_required: 1. A valid identity card i.e. Labour Card

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Construction workers registered with the Uttarakhand Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board are eligible.

faqs.question: What benefits are provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Medical check-up services and other preventive health services are provided.

faqs.question: How are the medical check-up facilities delivered?
faqs.answer: Facilities are provided through door-to-door visits, health camps, and collection centers.

faqs.question: Can unregistered workers avail of this facility?
faqs.answer: No, only registered construction workers are eligible for the scheme.

faqs.question: Can workers choose to visit collection centers instead of door-to-door services?
How does this scheme benefit construction workers?
faqs.answer: It promotes early detection of health issues, ensures preventive care, and contributes to overall health and productivity.
